summary_one_line: A SELECT body with a chained relational/arithmetic operator expression (e.g. comparison operators) reaching this sqlite3ExprCodeTarget op-codegen arm; bare SELECT 1 never does.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SELECT statement whose result expression is a chain of relational/arithmetic operators (the winning seed is `SELECT \t22&22%22>>22...`), so sqlite3ExprCodeTarget is invoked on an operator-node Expr that drives the codegen path through line 103568 on its TRUE side, rather than a trivial integer literal.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winning seed (value_profile_cmplog, 51 bytes) is `SELECT` followed by `22&22%22>>22222...` — a long expression with `&`, `%`, `>` operators feeding the expression-codegen walk, and the branch is hit (avg hitcount on branch winner=8, loser=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are bare/short queries (`SELECT 1;`, `SELECT 1:C:...`) with no operator-expression chain, so sqlite3ExprCodeTarget's operator arm at 103568 never takes its TRUE side (loser hitcount=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback: each integer/relational CMP inside the expression evaluation writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus, letting value_profile incrementally build the operator-laden expression that reaches the codegen arm. naive and plain cmplog lack the CMP_MAP distance feedback and never get the gradient reward, so they stall on trivial SELECTs.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the value_profile technique and both are won by the value-profile-carrying fuzzer.
The second decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog > cmplog, also delta value_profile.
The winning seed is a SELECT containing a chain of arithmetic/relational operators.
The loser-blocking seeds are trivial SELECT statements with no operator-expression chain.
The winner reaches the branch while the loser never does (hitcount divergence on the branch).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=8  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker's TRUE side is taken only in the winner-resolving role and is the globally blocked side.
value_profile feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operand pair is closer to matching by adding a new CMP_MAP bucket and preserving the corpus member.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the divergence is attributable to the CMP_MAP gradient feedback rather than I2S substitution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile delta), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S-floor lineage check does not apply; both decisive pairs are won by the value-profile-carrying fuzzer over its non-value-profile counterpart, and the winner-side fuzzer value_profile explicitly carries no I2SRandReplace per MECHANISM CONTEXT, so the attributable technique is the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating this op-codegen arm behind an operator-expression SELECT shows naive resolving as often as value_profile (no value-profile advantage), indicating CMP_MAP gradient feedback is not what drives reaching the branch.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is available, so the inference that the operator-chain expression (not merely a longer/non-trivial SELECT) is the required feature rests on a single example contrasted against trivial losers.
